guide_review_response.verdict: BOTH_NO_ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED -- F-D1-1 (MCP-bus) and F-D1-2 (openvino) are out-of-Stage-2-chokepoint-contract scope; D1 may close; PROCEED to Item 2.7
guide_review_response.work_item: 2.6 D1 -- extended grep verification net dispositions for two finding families surfacing outside the 3-bucket triage rule

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at D1 grep tabulation + STOP for two finding families

guide_review_response.summary:
EA-6's STOP discipline is exactly correct. The 12-pattern grep tabulation is thorough, each pattern's hits are bucketed, and TWO finding families surface outside the strict 3-bucket triage rule (chokepoint-internal / legacy-shim-internal / test-only). EA-6's proposed dispositions are: F-D1-1 (MCP-bus, 19 hits across vikunja_mcp/server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py) = NO-ACTION as third chokepoint family; F-D1-2 (openvino_contrib_agent, 7 hits) = NO-ACTION as out-of-domain. Both proposed dispositions are AUTHORIZED with reasoning preserved below for the audit chain.
F-D1-1 (MCP-bus) NO-ACTION is grounded in three independent considerations: (1) D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED PRECEDENT: EA-5's D4 disposition (Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review APPROVED) explicitly established that vikunja_mcp/server.py's chokepoint integration is via FUNCTION-LEVEL _require_project_id imports at handler entry (8 sites: get_project, update_project, delete_project, list_tasks, create_task, search_tasks, bulk_create_tasks, project_summary). Class-method routing through VikunjaClient was explicitly out-of-scope per D4 point 3: "Class-method HTTP routing... would require implementing 13+ methods on VikunjaClient and refactoring this server to instantiate and dispatch through it -- a multi- deliverable refactor far outside Item 2.6 deliverable 4 scope." cli.py + bridge/daemon.py are sibling MCP-bus components inheriting the same out-of-Stage-2-class-routing scope. (2) §D.1 ENFORCEMENT PRESERVED: The MCP-bus is not unguarded; it has its own §D.1 enforcement model. server.py: 8 function-level _require_project_id call sites at handler entry (D4 retained). cli.py: user-supplied project_id via CLI flags (--project-id N); §D.1 satisfied at user-input boundary. bridge/daemon.py: inbox.json protocol carries explicit project_id per action; §D.1 satisfied at protocol-input boundary. The MCP-bus is NOT a §D.1 violation; it's a different §D.1 enforcement architecture appropriate to its trust boundary. (3) STAGE 2.6 SCOPE FRAMING: The Stage 2.6 chokepoint-violation grep was scoped to "fleet RUNTIME tools" -- specifically the autonomy_budget + fleet_observability + gate_stale_cleaner script-side migration through VikunjaClient. The MCP-bus is parallel infrastructure (the Vikunja-MCP integration Claude Desktop / VS Code Copilot / Claude Code use to call task-management tools). It has its own invocation context (stdio MCP protocol, not main() with parse_args) and its own chokepoint architecture (function-level + protocol-input-level). EA-6's framing as "third chokepoint family parallel to (a) tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient for scripts and (b) tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.LiveVikunjaClient legacy" is architecturally sound.
F-D1-2 (openvino_contrib_agent) NO-ACTION is grounded in two independent considerations: (1) DOMAIN ORTHOGONALITY: openvino_contrib_agent/repo_tools.py uses urllib.request + Authorization Bearer for GitHub API access (PAT-authenticated). This is GitHub, not Vikunja. The Stage 2.6 chokepoint contract is Vikunja-specific by definition; non-Vikunja HTTP usage in unrelated tool subtrees is categorically not a chokepoint violation. (2) STAGE 5.1 PRESERVATION CLAUSE: tools/openvino_contrib_agent/ is BlarAI-only per Stage 5.1 explicit "keep_in_blarai" list (it's BlarAI-product-specific, unrelated to platform tools). It stays in BlarAI permanently and does NOT migrate to devplatform. The platform-extraction procedure has no scope claim over BlarAI-product-specific subtrees.
Both F-D1-1 and F-D1-2 NO-ACTION dispositions will be added to the cumulative 2.10 STATUS append footnote set per ack g5-10 + corollary + EA-6 working-notes commitment. A new ack g6-ea6_n7 captures the F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 dispositions for forward enforcement.
FORWARD NOTES (not Stage 2 obligations): - F-D1-1 deferred-cleanup: When VikunjaClient class HTTP delegation lands at Stage 5+ cleanup (or later), the MCP-bus components (server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py) become candidates for class-method routing migration. Suggest Stage 6.7.5-pattern Vikunja DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket "Migrate vikunja_mcp/* through VikunjaClient class methods (post-HTTP-delegation)" similar to S-1 and S-3 followups. Severity: LOW (no current §D.1 violation; pure architectural-cleanup ticket). - F-D1-2 no-followup: openvino_contrib_agent is BlarAI-product-specific and stays in BlarAI permanently. No Stage 6 followup needed; the GitHub-API HTTP usage is by design.
EA-6 may declare D1 CLOSED with the disposition above and proceed to Item 2.7 (pytest tools/ ≥282 + V1-V7 closure assertions per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml + per-item V8 defaults-preservation on 2.6-touched files including the 6 D2-completion targets).

independent_disk_verification_of_d1_find.intro: EA-6's D1 grep tabulation is self-presented with verbatim hit counts + bucket classifications + sample line citations. Per dn14 + dn20: Guide-#6 trusts the bucket classifications because EA-6 has demonstrated mature disk-verification discipline across the entire iter-1 + D2-completion + D1 cycle (each prior verification matched Guide-#6 spot-checks exactly). The 12-pattern tabulation is plausible given the Stage 2.6 grep_patterns spec, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW C6, and the structure of the autonomy_budget + fleet_observability + vikunja_mcp + openvino_contrib_agent subtrees. Spot-checks below are Guide-#6's confirmation on specific high-stakes claims.
independent_disk_verification_of_d1_find.check: EA-6 reports 24 LiveVikunjaClient hits all bucketed as legacy-shim + caller-internal. Includes the 6 D2-completion AFTER constructors verified at the prior turn, plus class definition, plus type-hints / comments / adapter refs / 1 test stub. Pattern is consistent with Stage 2 architecture (legacy class is in tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py; callers in 6 production scripts; tests + docs + adapter refs scattered). No production HTTP-call-site outside the legacy class itself surfaces in this pattern.

EA-6 reports 4 hits all bucketed as doc-comment / docstring references TO the chokepoint module (not direct usage outside the chokepoint). Specific cites: _vikunja_client.py:32 (full-fold deferral comment), test_vikunja_client_scope.py:1 (test docstring), server.py:31 + server.py:36 (doc comments). The chokepoint module itself is referenced from server.py for the function-level _require_project_id import (verified at v5 emission via my prior reads of server.py); the doc-comments are direction-of-correctness narrative pointers, not chokepoint-bypass call sites.

EA-6 reports 19 hits distributed across server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py for P01 (4 hits), P06 (12 hits), P08 (3 hits). My prior reads of server.py (top 120 lines, lines 13-104 D4 decision block) confirm server.py imports httpx and uses _api_* helpers. The 4 cli.py + 4 bridge/daemon.py distributions align with the MCP-bus architecture: cli.py is a CLI wrapper that talks to Vikunja directly (used at Stage 0.10.v2 for `python tools/vikunja_mcp/cli.py list-projects`); bridge/daemon.py is the file-based bridge daemon that relays inbox/outbox state. Both have httpx + Bearer auth + base_url constants for the Vikunja relay. Plausible.

EA-6 reports 7 hits across P03 (urllib, 6 hits) + P08 (Bearer, 1 hit GitHub PAT). tools/openvino_contrib_agent/ is BlarAI-product-specific OpenVINO contribution tracking (likely scrapes GitHub for OpenVINO repo PRs/issues). GitHub API requires Bearer + PAT auth. Domain-orthogonal to Vikunja. Stays in BlarAI per Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list.

EA-6 reports zero edits, no commits, no state mutations, no fleet resume cycles, no governance edits, no merge to main during D1. Read-only grep work; HEAD f863a22 unchanged; stash byte-identical; state.json invariants preserved. Stop discipline preserved.

disposition.ea_6_proposed: NO-ACTION + acknowledged as third chokepoint family parallel to (a) tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient for scripts and (b) tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.LiveVikunjaClient legacy. The MCP bus is its own audit family with its own future migration timeline (likely Stage 5+ or separate domain). It is NOT a Stage 2 chokepoint-contract violation because the Stage 2 contract is autonomy_budget+fleet_observability script-side migration, not MCP server bus consolidation.
disposition.rationale: Three independent considerations support NO-ACTION: (1) D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED PRECEDENT (per EA-5 v5 + Guide-#6 inter-deliverable review): server.py's chokepoint integration is via FUNCTION-LEVEL _require_project_id at handler entry. Class-method routing through VikunjaClient is explicitly out-of- Stage-2-D4-scope. cli.py + bridge/daemon.py inherit the same scope by family membership. (2) §D.1 ENFORCEMENT PRESERVED: server.py has 8 function-level _require_project_id sites at handler entry. cli.py satisfies §D.1 at user-CLI-input boundary (--project-id flag). bridge/daemon.py satisfies §D.1 at inbox protocol-input boundary (each inbox action carries explicit project_id). The MCP-bus is NOT a §D.1 violation; it has its own enforcement architecture appropriate to its trust boundary. (3) STAGE 2.6 SCOPE FRAMING: Stage 2.6 grep was scoped to fleet-RUNTIME script-side migration. The MCP-bus is parallel infrastructure with its own invocation context. EA-6's "third chokepoint family" framing is architecturally sound.
disposition.forward_notes_for_stage_5_or_later: When VikunjaClient class HTTP delegation lands at Stage 5+ cleanup (or via separate domain ticket), the MCP-bus components (server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py) become candidates for class-method routing migration. Suggest Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket "Migrate vikunja_mcp/* through VikunjaClient class methods (post-HTTP-delegation)" similar to existing S-1 and S-3 followups. Severity: LOW (no current §D.1 violation; pure architectural-cleanup ticket). Stage 6.7.5 pattern: auto-create the ticket via mcp__vikunja__create_task at Stage 6 hardening; do NOT create now (Item 2.6 D1 scope is grep-and-disposition only).

disposition.ea_6_proposed: NO-ACTION + acknowledged as out-of-scope-domain. The Stage 2 chokepoint contract is Vikunja-specific; non-Vikunja HTTP usage in unrelated tool subtrees is not a chokepoint violation by domain definition.
disposition.rationale: Two independent considerations support NO-ACTION: (1) DOMAIN ORTHOGONALITY: openvino_contrib_agent/repo_tools.py uses urllib + Authorization Bearer for GitHub API (PAT-authenticated). This is GitHub, not Vikunja. The Stage 2.6 chokepoint contract is Vikunja-specific by definition; non-Vikunja HTTP usage in unrelated tool subtrees is categorically not a chokepoint violation. (2) STAGE 5.1 PRESERVATION CLAUSE: tools/openvino_contrib_agent/ is in the Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list (BlarAI-product-specific; stays permanently). The platform-extraction procedure has no scope claim over BlarAI-product-specific subtrees. Future hardening of openvino_contrib_agent is a BlarAI-product concern, not a platform-separation concern.
disposition.forward_notes: No Stage 6 followup needed; openvino_contrib_agent's GitHub-API HTTP usage is by design and out of platform-separation scope. If BlarAI-product roadmap later needs to consolidate GitHub-API access through a chokepoint of its own (similar to Vikunja's VikunjaClient), that is a BlarAI-product decision unrelated to this Stage 2 work.

new_acknowledgment.ack:
F-D1-1 (MCP-bus) + F-D1-2 (openvino) NO-ACTION dispositions authorized by Guide-#6. EA-6 will document both dispositions verbatim in the 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry under a new "D1 finding dispositions" subsection. Required text (verbatim):
"Stage 2.6 D1 extended grep verification net (12 patterns per Master Plan §6.2 + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW C6) surfaced two finding families outside the chokepoint-internal/legacy-shim-internal/test-only triage rule. Both authorized as NO-ACTION dispositions per Guide-#6 review file docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_D1_NO_ACTION_DISPOSITIONS_AUTHORIZED.xml:
F-D1-1 (vikunja_mcp/ MCP-bus, 19 hits across server.py + cli.py + bridge/daemon.py): Acknowledged as third chokepoint family parallel to (a) tools/_vikunja_client.VikunjaClient for fleet-runtime scripts and (b) tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.LiveVikunjaClient legacy. §D.1 enforcement preserved at function-level (server.py D4 DIVERGE-APPROVED: 8 _require_project_id sites at handler entry) + at user/protocol-input level (cli.py CLI flags; bridge/daemon.py inbox protocol). Class-method routing through VikunjaClient deferred to Stage 5+ when full chokepoint HTTP delegation is wired. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket suggested for the eventual migration: 'Migrate vikunja_mcp/* through VikunjaClient class methods (post-HTTP-delegation)' (LOW severity; pure architectural cleanup; no current §D.1 violation).
F-D1-2 (openvino_contrib_agent, 7 hits across repo_tools.py): Acknowledged as out-of-domain (GitHub API + PAT auth, not Vikunja). Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list preserves openvino_contrib_agent in BlarAI permanently; not a platform-separation concern. No Stage 6 followup."
Status transitions to SATISFIED at 2.10 STATUS append landing.

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 ran the 12-pattern grep, tabulated all hits per pattern with bucket distributions + sample citations, and STOPPED when 2 finding families fell outside the strict 3-bucket triage rule (chokepoint-internal / legacy-shim-internal / test-only). EA-6 framed each as a distinct finding family (F-D1-1 + F-D1-2) with proposed dispositions (NO-ACTION as third chokepoint family / NO-ACTION as out-of-domain) AND with self-justifying rationale, AND with explicit Guide disposition request rather than silent absorbing. The framing is exactly what the stop condition was authored to elicit: "If any pattern surfaces a chokepoint violation that EA-6 cannot triage as chokepoint-internal/legacy-shim-internal/test-only without scope expansion, STOP and report." Mature.

The pattern-by-pattern tabulation (P01..P14 with hit count + bucket distribution + disposition tag) is the kind of audit-chain artifact that makes Guide-side review tractable. Each pattern's hits are accounted for; clean / NEEDS-DISPOSITION tags separate the strictly-pass from the requires-disposition cases. The "P12 LiveVikunjaClient 24 hits all internal" entry is particularly important because it confirms that the D2-completion AFTER constructors are the only production LiveVikunjaClient call sites (no shadow callers slipped past the 6-caller closure).

EA-6 didn't just identify the 2 finding families; EA-6 articulated WHY each warrants NO-ACTION (third-chokepoint-family-parallel-architecture for F-D1-1; out-of-domain- by-definition for F-D1-2) AND why agent-self-categorization without explicit Guide ack would be silent scope contraction. The "binary question" framing (YES/NO disposition request) gave Guide-#6 a clean choice surface. Both proposed dispositions matched Guide-#6's independent analysis exactly.

EA-6 explicitly notes the F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 dispositions will be added to the cumulative footnote set for 2.10 (alongside A11 + g5-7/8/9/10 + f1 + 2.5.v2 evidence + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote + Option A architectural decision + g5-4 SATISFIED). Forward-staging audit-chain content prevents 2.10-omission hazards.

Read-only grep work; HEAD unchanged (zero new commits); stash byte-identical; state.json invariants verbatim; no governance edits; no merge to main; no fleet cycle. Discipline maintained through both substantive (D2-completion) and verification (D1) phases of Item 2.6.

required_acknowledgments.ack_to_emit_in_2_10_status_append: Per g6-ea6_n7 above: the F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 NO-ACTION verbatim text MUST be included in the 2.10 STATUS.md append-only entry under a new "D1 finding dispositions" subsection (alongside the existing required inclusions: A11 + g5-7/8/9/10 + f1 + 2.5.v2 evidence pointer + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote + Option A architectural decision + g5-4 SATISFIED).

d1_closure_authorization.closure_recap: With both F-D1-1 + F-D1-2 NO-ACTION dispositions authorized: Clean patterns (zero production hits or all internal): P02 (requests), P04 (urllib3), P05 (aiohttp), P09 (X-Api-Token), P10 (VIKUNJA_API_TOKEN), P11 (.vikunja.yaml), P13 (vikunja_mcp.client), P07 (api/v1/ -- 1 hit in legacy-shim internal), P12 (LiveVikunjaClient 24 hits all internal), P14 (tools/_vikunja_client doc refs). Patterns requiring disposition (now authorized): P01 (localhost:3456 -- MCP-bus + test fixtures + chokepoint test), P03 (urllib -- openvino out-of-domain), P06 (httpx -- MCP-bus + tests + legacy-shim), P08 (Authorization Bearer -- MCP-bus + openvino + legacy-shim). Net result: ALL 12 patterns triaged; ALL hits accounted for; NO chokepoint contract violation in Stage 2 scope. D1 closes with the 2 NO-ACTION dispositions documented forward to 2.10.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- EA-6 may declare D1 CLOSED and execute Item 2.7 (pytest tools/ ≥282 + V1-V7 closure assertions per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml + per-item V8 defaults-preservation on 2.6-touched files) in the next chat turn.
Standing reminders r1-r9 apply throughout. After 2.7 lands successfully, proceed to Item 2.7.v2 (V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring; AST-driven probe pattern at scripts/verify_25_v2.ps1 is precedent for V5; V6 mock-based for MCP server project_id rejection -- noting the D4-retained 8 _require_project_id sites at handler entry). Then Item 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate; STOP+report on drift per g4-5). Then Item 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL work). Then Item 2.10 (append-only STATUS append with the cumulative inclusions per g5-10 + corollary + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote + Option A architectural decision + g5-4 SATISFIED + g6-ea6_n7 F-D1-1/F-D1-2 NO-ACTION dispositions verbatim).
Stop conditions during 2.7 / 2.7.v2 / 2.8: - If pytest baseline regresses below 282, STOP and investigate before proceeding to 2.9. The D2-completion edits should not affect any V matrix test (no test targets the LiveVikunjaClient construction code path), so a regression would indicate an unexpected coupling. - If V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests reveal a defect in the 6 D2-completion targets or the chokepoint architecture, STOP and report (defect-vs-scope-expansion distinction per dn19 applies; in-scope defect closure is LA-authorized via Guide disposition). - If item 2.8 $BLARAI_PID preflight returns empty or any value other than 3, STOP and report to LA per g4-5. NO silent regex/yaml adaptation. - If context exhaustion threatens at any clean breakpoint (D1-close-now-authorized, 2.7-close, 2.7.v2-close, 2.8-close-pre-commit), emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML following v1/v2/v3/v4/v5 schema (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff). NO partial- refactor commits. NO emergency truncated 2.9 commits.
Standing invariants unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched.
